prompts.title: Solve DC Circuit Using KVL and KCL
prompts.prompt: Act as an EEE professor with 15 years experience teaching Network Analysis at NIT Trichy. I need you to solve a DC circuit problem using Kirchhoff's Voltage Law (KVL) and Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL), step by step, in the exact style expected in Indian university semester exams and GATE EE. Here is the context: I will paste my circuit description below (components, values in ohms, voltage sources in volts, current sources in amps, and node/mesh labels). If I have a circuit diagram, I will describe it branch-by-branch. Please provide: (1) a clearly labelled node/mesh assumption with sign conventions, (2) each KVL loop equation and each KCL node equation written out symbolically first then with numbers substituted, (3) the full matrix form Ax=b with the solved values of branch currents and node voltages, (4) a power balance check showing total power delivered equals total power absorbed (within 0.1 percent), (5) common mistakes an Indian 2nd-year EEE student makes on this type of problem. Format the output as numbered sections with equations in plain text (use * for multiplication, / for division). Consider these constraints: align terminology with Sudhakar and Shyammohan (standard Indian textbook), show at least one mesh and one nodal method, and flag if the circuit has a supernode or supermesh. My circuit details: [PASTE YOUR CIRCUIT HERE]

prompts.title: Explain Three-Phase Power for Indian Grid
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior power systems engineer with 12 years experience at POSOCO handling the Indian 400kV/765kV grid. I need you to explain three-phase AC power fundamentals to a 3rd-year EEE student at Anna University preparing for GATE and a core-sector placement at PGCIL. Here is the context: the student knows single-phase RMS and phasors but is confused about line-to-line vs phase quantities, star vs delta, and why India uses 3-phase 50 Hz at 11kV/33kV/66kV/132kV/220kV/400kV/765kV levels. Please provide: (1) derivation of VL = sqrt(3) * Vph and IL relationships for star and delta with phasor diagrams described in words, (2) active, reactive and apparent power formulas P = sqrt(3) VL IL cos(phi) with a worked example at 11kV, 500kVA, 0.85 pf lagging typical of an Indian industrial feeder, (3) why 50 Hz and these specific voltage levels are used per CEA regulations and IS 12360, (4) the role of neutral grounding in Indian distribution (solidly grounded vs resistance grounded) and relevance to DISCOM faults, (5) 5 GATE EE style MCQs with solutions. Format as a structured tutorial with headings, equations in plain text, and a final summary table mapping voltage level to typical use case (generation, transmission, sub-transmission, distribution, utilization). Consider these constraints: use BIS/IS standards, POSOCO terminology, and realistic Indian load examples.

prompts.title: Thevenin and Norton Equivalent Walkthrough
prompts.prompt: Act as a network theory tutor with 10 years experience coaching GATE EE aspirants at Made Easy Delhi. I need you to compute Thevenin and Norton equivalents for a linear circuit I provide and teach the method so I can replicate it in exams. Here is the context: I am a 2nd-year EEE student at DTU. I will paste my circuit with resistor values in ohms, independent and dependent sources, and the two terminals A-B across which the equivalent is required. Please provide: (1) step-by-step computation of Vth (open-circuit voltage) using nodal analysis, (2) Rth using the test-source method (applying 1V or 1A) since dependent sources may be present, (3) In (short-circuit current) and verification that Vth/In = Rth, (4) the final Thevenin and Norton equivalent circuits drawn in ASCII, (5) maximum power transferable to a load RL = Rth with numerical value in watts, (6) a tabular comparison of the three methods and when to prefer each in a 3-hour GATE paper. Format as numbered steps with equations in plain text. Consider these constraints: follow the sign convention used in Van Valkenburg and Sudhakar, assume ideal op-amps are not present unless stated, and call out any pitfalls with dependent sources. My circuit: [PASTE CIRCUIT]

prompts.title: RLC Transient Response Derivation
prompts.prompt: Act as a circuits professor with 18 years experience at IIT Madras teaching EE2010 Network Theory. I need you to derive and plot the transient response of a series or parallel RLC circuit for the values I provide. Here is the context: I will give R in ohms, L in henrys, C in farads, initial conditions iL(0) and vC(0), and the forcing function (step, impulse, or sinusoid). I am preparing for a semester exam at VIT Vellore. Please provide: (1) the governing second-order ODE derived from KVL/KCL, (2) characteristic equation, damping ratio zeta, natural frequency omega_n, and classification as overdamped, critically damped, or underdamped, (3) closed-form time-domain solution with constants evaluated from initial conditions, (4) values of rise time, peak time, percent overshoot, and settling time (2 percent) if underdamped, (5) a text-based sketch of vC(t) or iL(t) waveform with key points annotated, (6) a MATLAB/Octave script using tf and step to cross-verify. Format as numbered sections with the ODE and solution in plain-text math. Consider these constraints: match Indian university semester exam rigor, reference the Laplace transform approach as an alternative, and note that Indian 50 Hz sources mean omega = 100*pi rad/s. My RLC values: [PASTE VALUES]

prompts.title: Laplace Transform Circuit Analysis
prompts.prompt: Act as a signals and systems instructor with 12 years teaching experience at BITS Pilani. I need you to analyze a circuit in the s-domain using Laplace transforms for the problem I provide. Here is the context: I will describe a circuit with resistors, capacitors, inductors (with initial conditions), and a voltage or current source that may be a step, ramp, exponential, or sinusoid. I want the transfer function H(s) from input to a specified output and the time-domain response. Please provide: (1) s-domain equivalent of each element including initial condition sources (L*iL(0) and vC(0)/s), (2) nodal or mesh equations in the s-domain, (3) algebraic solution for the output in s-domain, (4) partial fraction expansion and inverse Laplace to get the time-domain response, (5) poles and zeros with stability comment, (6) a brief Python SymPy snippet that reproduces the algebra. Format as numbered sections with plain-text equations using s, exp, sin, cos notation. Consider these constraints: follow Oppenheim and Willsky conventions used widely in Indian curricula, highlight ROC only if bilateral, and flag any pole-zero cancellation. My circuit and inputs: [PASTE HERE]

prompts.title: Power Flow Study Using Gauss-Seidel
prompts.prompt: Act as a power systems faculty member with 20 years experience at IIT Roorkee, who has consulted for PGCIL on load-flow studies. I need you to solve a small power-flow problem using the Gauss-Seidel method, exactly as expected in an Indian 7th-semester EEE power systems course. Here is the context: I will give a 3-bus or 4-bus system with bus types (slack, PV, PQ), line admittances in pu on a 100 MVA base, specified real and reactive powers, voltage magnitude limits, and starting guesses. Please provide: (1) the Ybus matrix constructed element-by-element, (2) the Gauss-Seidel update equation derived from Sk = Vk * conj(sum(Ykm*Vm)), (3) at least 3 iterations with complex voltages tabulated to 4 decimal places and mismatch check, (4) convergence criterion 1e-4 pu and final bus voltages and angles, (5) line flows and total system losses in MW and MVAR, (6) a comparison note on why Newton-Raphson would converge faster for this case. Format as iteration tables and equations. Consider these constraints: use pu system on 100 MVA base, follow IEEE/CEA conventions, and reference standard texts by Nagrath-Kothari and Hadi Saadat. My system data: [PASTE BUS AND LINE DATA]

prompts.title: Symmetrical Components Fault Analysis
prompts.prompt: Act as a protection engineer with 15 years experience at ABB India designing relay settings for 220kV substations. I need you to solve a symmetrical components fault problem for a grounded star generator feeding an Indian transmission line. Here is the context: I will provide generator sequence reactances X1, X2, X0 in pu on a 100 MVA base, neutral grounding impedance Zn, line sequence impedances, pre-fault voltage (1.0 pu), and the fault type (LG, LL, LLG, or 3-phase). Please provide: (1) sequence network diagrams described in text for each fault type, (2) derivation of fault current formulas starting from boundary conditions, (3) numerical fault currents in pu and amps at an 11kV base, (4) line-to-ground voltages at the fault point, (5) comparison of magnitudes showing why LG fault is usually the most common and 3-phase fault gives the largest symmetrical current, (6) relay pickup setting suggestion per IS/IEC 60255 for an overcurrent scheme. Format as tables and equations. Consider these constraints: use CEA grid code conventions, IS standards, and typical Indian 11kV/33kV distribution values. My system data: [PASTE IMPEDANCES AND FAULT TYPE]

prompts.title: Transformer Equivalent Circuit and Efficiency
prompts.prompt: Act as a transformer design engineer with 14 years experience at BHEL Bhopal. I need you to compute the equivalent circuit parameters, voltage regulation, and efficiency of a single-phase transformer from OC and SC test data I provide. Here is the context: I will give rated kVA, primary and secondary voltages, frequency 50 Hz, OC test readings (V1, I0, W0) on LV side, SC test readings (Vsc, Isc, Wsc) on HV side, and load power factor. I am a 4th-year EEE student at COEP Pune. Please provide: (1) no-load parameters R0 and X0 referred to LV, (2) equivalent series impedance Req and Xeq referred to HV and LV, (3) approximate equivalent circuit diagram in text, (4) voltage regulation at full load at 0.8 lagging, unity, and 0.8 leading power factor, (5) efficiency at 25, 50, 75, 100 and 125 percent loading and load at which maximum efficiency occurs, (6) all-day efficiency for an Indian distribution transformer duty cycle I will provide. Format as tables with formulas. Consider these constraints: conform to IS 2026 and IS 1180 for distribution transformers, use per-unit where helpful. My test data: [PASTE]

prompts.title: Induction Motor Torque-Slip Characteristics
prompts.prompt: Act as an electrical machines professor with 16 years experience at NIT Warangal. I need you to derive and plot the torque-slip characteristic of a 3-phase squirrel cage induction motor using the values I provide. Here is the context: I will give rated voltage, frequency 50 Hz, pole count, stator and rotor referred impedances R1, X1, R2', X2', magnetizing reactance Xm, and rated power. Please provide: (1) the approximate equivalent circuit with Thevenin equivalent looking into the rotor, (2) torque expression T = (3/omega_s) * Vth^2 * (R2'/s) / ((Rth + R2'/s)^2 + (Xth + X2')^2), (3) starting torque, maximum torque and slip at max torque, (4) a table of torque vs slip for s = 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1.0, (5) a text sketch of the torque-slip curve marking stable and unstable regions, (6) effect of rotor resistance insertion used in Indian crane and hoist applications typical of L&T and Siemens India drives. Format as equations and tables. Consider these constraints: reference IS 12615 for energy-efficient motors, assume 415V 50Hz Indian supply. My motor data: [PASTE]

prompts.title: Synchronous Generator Performance Analysis
prompts.prompt: Act as a generation engineer with 18 years experience at NTPC Ramagundam on 500 MW synchronous generators. I need you to compute the performance of a cylindrical rotor synchronous generator from the data I provide. Here is the context: I will give rated MVA, voltage, power factor, synchronous reactance Xs, armature resistance Ra, and operating load. I am a final-year EEE student at VNIT Nagpur preparing for PSU interviews. Please provide: (1) phasor diagram construction for lagging, unity and leading pf with E, V, IaRa, IaXs drops labelled, (2) excitation EMF E magnitude and load angle delta, (3) voltage regulation by synchronous impedance method, (4) active and reactive power equations P = (EV/Xs) sin(delta), Q = (EV cos(delta) - V^2)/Xs with numerical values, (5) steady-state stability limit and comment on operation near it, (6) V-curves and compound curves concept with an Indian grid context (reactive power support to POSOCO). Format as equations, a tabulated phasor, and plain-text diagrams. Consider these constraints: use IS 4722 and IEC 60034 conventions, assume 50 Hz. My generator data: [PASTE]

prompts.title: PID Controller Tuning Using Ziegler-Nichols
prompts.prompt: Act as a control systems engineer with 12 years experience at Honeywell Automation India tuning loops in refineries. I need you to tune a PID controller for a plant whose transfer function or step response I provide, using Ziegler-Nichols and Cohen-Coon methods. Here is the context: I am a 6th-semester EEE student at PSG Tech doing my control systems lab. I will give G(s) = K * exp(-Ls) / (tau*s + 1) parameters or raw step response data points (time vs output). Please provide: (1) identification of process gain K, dead time L, and time constant tau from the step response, (2) Ziegler-Nichols open-loop PID gains Kp, Ki, Kd (and Ti, Td), (3) Cohen-Coon PID gains for comparison, (4) closed-loop step response characteristics (overshoot, settling time, steady-state error) predicted for each tuning, (5) a MATLAB/Simulink or Python control snippet that simulates both, (6) practical notes on derivative kick, integral windup, and filter time constant N typical of Honeywell DCS. Format as tables and code block. Consider these constraints: assume sensor and actuator saturations common in Indian process plants, and discuss anti-windup. My plant data: [PASTE]

prompts.title: Bode Plot and Stability Margins
prompts.prompt: Act as a control systems lecturer with 10 years experience at IIT Hyderabad. I need you to draw a straight-line Bode plot for an open-loop transfer function G(s)H(s) I provide and assess closed-loop stability. Here is the context: I will give G(s)H(s) as a ratio of polynomials in s, possibly with delay. I need this for a semester exam at Jadavpur University. Please provide: (1) factorization into constant, poles/zeros at origin, first-order, and second-order terms, (2) corner frequencies and slope contributions at each, (3) straight-line asymptotic magnitude plot in dB and phase plot in degrees across 0.1 to 1000 rad/s described as a tabulated plot, (4) gain crossover frequency, phase crossover frequency, gain margin and phase margin with stability verdict, (5) bandwidth and resonant peak estimate, (6) a Python control snippet using control.bode to cross-check. Format as a frequency table followed by equations and code. Consider these constraints: follow Ogata and Nagrath-Gopal conventions used in Indian curricula, and treat any transport delay via Pade approximation. My G(s)H(s): [PASTE]

prompts.title: Root Locus Design for Second-Order Plant
prompts.prompt: Act as a control systems mentor with 14 years of experience at ISRO VSSC designing attitude control loops. I need you to sketch the root locus for a unity feedback system with open-loop transfer function G(s)H(s) I provide and design a controller to meet given time-domain specs. Here is the context: I will give G(s)H(s) and target specs (damping ratio zeta, settling time ts, peak overshoot Mp). I am a 7th-semester EEE student at MNIT Jaipur. Please provide: (1) all root locus rules applied: number of branches, asymptotes, centroid, breakaway/break-in points, jw-axis crossings, angles of departure/arrival, (2) text sketch of root locus with key points labelled, (3) value of gain K for the desired closed-loop pole location computed via magnitude condition, (4) lead or lag compensator design if specs cannot be met by gain alone, (5) final closed-loop transfer function and simulated step response parameters, (6) a MATLAB rlocus and rlocfind snippet. Format as step list and code. Consider these constraints: use Evans rules as in Nagrath-Gopal, verify via Routh-Hurwitz if a branch crosses jw-axis. My plant and specs: [PASTE]

prompts.title: State-Space Controller and Observer Design
prompts.prompt: Act as a modern control theory professor with 15 years experience at IISc Bangalore. I need you to design a full-state feedback controller and a Luenberger observer for a linear system I provide. Here is the context: I will give A, B, C, D matrices of a continuous-time system and desired closed-loop pole locations for both the controller and the observer. Please provide: (1) controllability matrix and rank check, (2) observability matrix and rank check, (3) state feedback gain K using Ackermann's formula or pole placement with step-by-step computation, (4) observer gain L using the dual design, (5) combined observer-based compensator state-space representation and separation principle justification, (6) a Python control or MATLAB snippet using place() to cross-check and simulate the step response from non-zero initial conditions. Format as matrices in plain text (row by row) followed by code. Consider these constraints: ensure observer poles are 3 to 5 times faster than controller poles, reference Ogata Modern Control Engineering used at Indian universities, and note the practical issue of sensor noise amplification. My system matrices and specs: [PASTE]

prompts.title: FIR Filter Design Using Window Method
prompts.prompt: Act as a DSP engineer with 10 years experience at Texas Instruments India designing audio codecs. I need you to design an FIR filter using the window method based on specs I provide and help me understand the procedure for my DSP lab at SRM Chennai. Here is the context: I will give filter type (LP/HP/BP/BS), sampling frequency fs in Hz, passband and stopband edge frequencies, passband ripple and stopband attenuation. Please provide: (1) selection of the appropriate window (rectangular, Hamming, Hanning, Blackman, Kaiser) based on attenuation requirement, (2) filter order N computation from transition width, (3) ideal impulse response hd[n] formula and numerical values, (4) window w[n] values, (5) final h[n] = hd[n]*w[n] coefficients tabulated, (6) Python/SciPy snippet using scipy.signal.firwin and freqz to verify magnitude response in dB. Format as tables of coefficients and a code block. Consider these constraints: use Proakis and Manolakis conventions followed in Indian DSP courses, ensure linear phase (symmetric coefficients), and note fixed-point implementation issues for a C6000 DSP or STM32. My filter specs: [PASTE]

prompts.title: IIR Filter Design via Bilinear Transform
prompts.prompt: Act as a signal processing instructor with 12 years experience at IIT Kharagpur. I need you to design an IIR digital filter using the bilinear transform from an analog prototype for specs I provide. Here is the context: I will give filter type, sampling frequency, passband/stopband edges and ripples. I am preparing for my 6th-sem DSP end-sem at Anna University. Please provide: (1) prewarp the critical frequencies using omega = (2/T)*tan(w*T/2), (2) analog prototype order and transfer function (Butterworth, Chebyshev I/II, or Elliptic) with numerical poles and zeros, (3) bilinear transformation s = (2/T)*(1-z^-1)/(1+z^-1) applied step by step to yield H(z), (4) difference equation y[n] = ... for direct form II implementation, (5) pole-zero plot description and stability check (all poles inside unit circle), (6) Python/SciPy verification snippet using scipy.signal.iirfilter and freqz. Format with equations and code. Consider these constraints: follow Oppenheim-Schafer notation, mention bilinear transform does not preserve impulse response but preserves stability, and highlight quantization concerns for 16-bit fixed point on STM32. My IIR specs: [PASTE]

prompts.title: Fourier Series and Spectrum of a Signal
prompts.prompt: Act as a signals and systems tutor with 8 years experience teaching at VIT Vellore. I need you to compute the Fourier series representation of a periodic signal I describe and interpret its spectrum. Here is the context: I will describe the signal (square, triangular, sawtooth, half-wave rectified sine, full-wave rectified sine, or arbitrary piecewise definition) with period T and amplitude A. I am a 4th-semester EEE student. Please provide: (1) symmetry analysis (even, odd, half-wave) to eliminate unnecessary coefficients, (2) trigonometric Fourier series coefficients a0, an, bn derived from integrals shown step by step, (3) exponential Fourier series coefficients cn, (4) magnitude and phase spectrum tabulated for n = 0 to 10 harmonics, (5) Parseval's power check and THD calculation relevant to power quality in Indian grids, (6) a Python numpy snippet reconstructing the signal from the first 20 harmonics and comparing with the original. Format as integrals, tables, and code. Consider these constraints: express answers with pi symbolically and numerically, and relate harmonic content to IEEE 519 / IEC 61000 power-quality standards adopted by CEA. My signal: [DESCRIBE]

prompts.title: DFT and FFT Computation Walkthrough
prompts.prompt: Act as a DSP lab instructor with 9 years experience at NIT Surathkal. I need you to compute the N-point DFT of a discrete sequence I provide, then explain the radix-2 FFT butterfly structure. Here is the context: I will provide a sequence x[n] of length N (where N is a power of 2, typically 4 or 8) for my 5th-sem DSP lab at BITS Pilani. Please provide: (1) the DFT definition X[k] = sum_{n=0}^{N-1} x[n] * exp(-j*2*pi*k*n/N) with each X[k] computed explicitly and shown in rectangular and polar form, (2) the twiddle factors W_N^kn tabulated, (3) the decimation-in-time radix-2 FFT flow graph described step by step with stage-wise intermediate values, (4) operation count comparison: DFT needs N^2 complex mults vs FFT needs (N/2)*log2(N), (5) magnitude and phase spectrum plotted as a text table, (6) Python numpy.fft.fft verification snippet. Format as matrices, tables, and code. Consider these constraints: follow Proakis conventions, highlight the bit-reversal input order for DIT FFT, and mention how this scales for a 1024-point FFT on ARM CMSIS-DSP. My sequence: [PASTE]

prompts.title: Z-Transform and Inverse Computation
prompts.prompt: Act as a DSP professor with 13 years experience at IIIT Hyderabad. I need you to compute the Z-transform or inverse Z-transform for a sequence or X(z) I provide, with full steps suitable for a GATE EE aspirant. Here is the context: I will give either x[n] in closed form or a tabulated finite sequence, or X(z) as a rational function. Please provide: (1) direct application of the Z-transform definition X(z) = sum x[n] z^-n or lookup from a standard table with each term justified, (2) region of convergence (ROC) with causality/stability interpretation, (3) for inverse: partial fraction expansion, long division, or residue method with full steps, (4) pole-zero plot description, (5) final x[n] expressed with u[n] step functions and numerical values for n = 0 to 10, (6) Python SymPy or scipy.signal snippet for verification. Format as equations and tables. Consider these constraints: reference Oppenheim-Schafer and Proakis as standard Indian texts, clearly mark right-sided vs two-sided sequences, and flag when partial fractions need manipulation for proper form. My input: [PASTE]

prompts.title: STM32 GPIO Blink Code With HAL
prompts.prompt: Act as an embedded systems mentor with 10 years experience at STMicroelectronics India training students on the STM32 ecosystem. I need you to write beginner-friendly STM32 HAL code that blinks an LED and explain every line. Here is the context: I am a 5th-semester EEE student at Manipal using an STM32F103C8T6 Blue Pill or STM32F411 Nucleo board. I want to blink an LED on PC13 (Blue Pill built-in) or PA5 (Nucleo) at 1 Hz using STM32CubeIDE and HAL. Please provide: (1) step-by-step STM32CubeMX configuration: clock tree at 72 MHz (Blue Pill) or 100 MHz (Nucleo), GPIO output push-pull, no pull, low speed, (2) complete main.c listing with SystemClock_Config(), MX_GPIO_Init(), and the while(1) loop using HAL_GPIO_TogglePin and HAL_Delay(500), (3) line-by-line comments, (4) how to flash using ST-Link V2 (very common in India, available on Robu.in for Rs 300), (5) common errors (wrong boot0 jumper, missing BOOT0 pull, incorrect target selection), (6) an exercise extension using a timer interrupt instead of HAL_Delay. Format as code blocks and numbered steps. Consider these constraints: use HAL, not LL or bare metal, and assume Windows 11 with STM32CubeIDE 1.14.

prompts.title: Arduino Interfacing Project Code
prompts.prompt: Act as an embedded hobbyist turned instructor with 8 years experience running Arduino workshops across Indian engineering colleges. I need you to write complete, well-commented Arduino code for a sensor interfacing project I describe. Here is the context: I am a 3rd-semester EEE student at SSN College of Engineering Chennai. I will tell you the sensor (DHT11, MPU6050, HC-SR04, LDR, LM35, or similar), the Arduino board (Uno R3, Nano, or Mega), and the desired output (serial monitor, LCD 16x2 via I2C, or OLED SSD1306). Please provide: (1) wiring table with Arduino pins, sensor pins, pull-ups, and voltage levels, (2) list of libraries needed from the Arduino Library Manager with exact names, (3) complete .ino sketch with setup() and loop() and inline comments in simple English, (4) calibration routine if the sensor needs it, (5) expected serial output example, (6) a small troubleshooting table (sensor reads 0, NaN, I2C address issue) with fixes. Format as a BOM table, wiring table, and code block. Consider these constraints: prefer modules easily available on Robu.in and Amazon India under Rs 500, use 5V logic for Uno and flag any 3.3V sensors needing a level shifter. My project: [DESCRIBE]

prompts.title: UART Communication on STM32 Tutorial
prompts.prompt: Act as an embedded firmware engineer with 11 years experience at Tata Elxsi working on automotive ECUs. I need you to teach me UART communication on an STM32F4 using HAL with a complete, working example. Here is the context: I am a 6th-semester EEE student at COEP Pune. I want to send Hello from STM32 every second over UART2 at 115200 8N1 and also echo back any characters received using interrupts. Please provide: (1) CubeMX setup: USART2 async mode, 115200 baud, PA2 TX and PA3 RX, NVIC enabled for USART2 global interrupt, (2) complete main.c with HAL_UART_Transmit in the main loop and HAL_UART_Receive_IT for reception, (3) the HAL_UART_RxCpltCallback implementation with a ring buffer for safety, (4) how to verify using Tera Term or PuTTY on Windows with a CP2102 USB-TTL adapter available for Rs 150 on Robu.in, (5) common bugs: wrong TX/RX cross wiring, ground not shared, baud mismatch, missing HAL_UART_Receive_IT re-enable in callback, (6) tips for DMA-based reception for high throughput. Format as step list and code blocks with comments. Consider these constraints: STM32CubeIDE 1.14, HAL based, and 3.3V logic.

prompts.title: I2C Sensor Driver in C
prompts.prompt: Act as a firmware engineer with 9 years experience at Bosch India writing I2C drivers for automotive sensors. I need you to write a clean, portable I2C driver in C for a specific sensor on an STM32 or Arduino. Here is the context: I am a final-year EEE student at IIT BHU building a weather station project. I will tell you the sensor (BMP280, BME280, MPU6050, SHT31, or ADS1115) and the MCU. Please provide: (1) a summary of the sensor's I2C address, register map highlights, and power-on sequence from the datasheet, (2) a platform-independent sensor_read(uint8_t reg, uint8_t *buf, uint8_t len) and sensor_write() wrapper using HAL_I2C_Mem_Read/Write on STM32 or Wire.h on Arduino, (3) an init() function that configures sampling, oversampling, and filters per datasheet, (4) a get_temperature_celsius() and get_pressure_pascal() function with the full compensation formulas from the datasheet, (5) error handling with return codes, (6) main loop usage example printing readings every 1 second. Format as a C header plus C source plus usage example. Consider these constraints: avoid floating-point where integer math suffices, account for 3.3V vs 5V logic, and add pull-up sizing note (typically 4.7k on 3.3V I2C).

prompts.title: FreeRTOS Task Scheduling Example
prompts.prompt: Act as an RTOS trainer with 13 years experience at Wipro PARI teaching FreeRTOS on Cortex-M MCUs. I need you to write a complete FreeRTOS example for an STM32F4 that has multiple tasks, a queue, and a semaphore. Here is the context: I am a 7th-semester EEE student at VIT Vellore doing a real-time systems project. I want three tasks: Task_Sensor (reads a mock ADC every 100 ms and pushes to a queue), Task_Process (takes from queue and filters), Task_Display (prints over UART every 500 ms), protected by a binary semaphore guarding the UART resource. Please provide: (1) CubeMX FreeRTOS middleware enablement with CMSIS-RTOS v2, (2) task stack sizes and priorities rationale, (3) complete freertos.c or app_freertos.c code with osThreadNew, osMessageQueueNew, osSemaphoreNew calls, (4) the three task functions with proper vTaskDelay-equivalent osDelay, (5) explanation of priority inversion risk and how the semaphore avoids garbled UART prints, (6) tips for tracing using SystemView or tracealyzer. Format as code blocks and a task table. Consider these constraints: configTICK_RATE_HZ = 1000, preemptive scheduling, and HAL-safe practices (no HAL_Delay inside tasks).

prompts.title: PCB Schematic Review Checklist
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior PCB design engineer with 16 years experience at Tejas Networks Bengaluru reviewing schematics before layout. I need you to review my schematic description and give a thorough checklist-based critique. Here is the context: I am a final-year EEE student at RVCE designing a 4-layer STM32F4 board with USB, CAN, a buck converter (TPS54531), and 3.3V LDO. I will paste a Bill of Materials and net description. Please provide: (1) power tree review: input protection (TVS, fuse), reverse polarity (P-MOSFET), inrush, decoupling count (100nF per VDD pin, 10uF bulk), (2) MCU essentials: BOOT0 pull-down, NRST RC, SWD header, HSE crystal load caps, VBAT, (3) USB: ESD protection (USBLC6-2), series resistors, D+ pull-up if full-speed, (4) CAN: transceiver selection (TJA1051), common-mode choke, split termination, 120 ohm terminators, (5) buck converter: feedback resistor values matching Vout, inductor saturation current check, Cin/Cout rating, bootstrap cap, (6) a numbered pass/fail checklist with specific net names from my BOM to flag. Format as a markdown-style checklist. Consider these constraints: components should be sourceable from Mouser India or Element14 India, and comply with IS/IEC creepage/clearance for 5V domain. My BOM: [PASTE]

prompts.title: KiCad PCB Layout Best Practices
prompts.prompt: Act as a hardware design mentor with 12 years experience using KiCad at an Indian hardware startup (InCore Semiconductors). I need you to produce a layout best-practices guide for a mixed-signal 2-layer or 4-layer PCB I am about to route in KiCad 7. Here is the context: I am a 7th-sem EEE student at IIT Dharwad. My board has an STM32, an ADC (ADS1115), an I2C sensor, a UART-USB bridge (CP2102), and a 3.3V LDO from a 5V USB input. Please provide: (1) layer stack-up recommendation (signal-GND-PWR-signal for 4-layer) and tradeoffs vs 2-layer, (2) component placement order and rationale (connectors, MCU, crystal near MCU, decoupling within 2 mm of pins), (3) ground strategy: single solid plane, avoid splits except for analog island under the ADC with a single-point tie, (4) high-speed routing: USB differential pair impedance 90 ohm, matched length, avoid vias; crystal traces short and guard-ringed, (5) power routing: buck inductor loop minimization, thermal reliefs, plane pours, (6) DRC and manufacturing checklist for JLCPCB/PCBPower India (minimum 6 mil trace/space, 0.3 mm via, 1 oz copper). Format as numbered guide with reasoning. Consider these constraints: KiCad 7 terminology and JLCPCB DFM rules.

prompts.title: Buck Converter Design Calculations
prompts.prompt: Act as a power electronics design engineer with 14 years experience at Delta Electronics India designing DC-DC converters. I need you to walk me through the full design of a synchronous buck converter for specs I provide. Here is the context: I am a 6th-semester EEE student at NIT Calicut. I will give Vin range, Vout, Iout_max, switching frequency fsw, target efficiency, and output ripple. Please provide: (1) duty cycle D = Vout/Vin and its variation across input range, (2) inductor L = Vout*(1-D)/(delta_IL*fsw) with delta_IL = 0.3*Iout_max; pick a standard value and check saturation current rating, (3) output capacitor Cout from ripple spec considering ESR; recommend X7R MLCCs or low-ESR electrolytics, (4) MOSFET conduction and switching loss estimates, (5) IC recommendation from TI (TPS54xx) or Richtek readily available in India with justification, (6) feedback compensator design (type II or type III) with crossover fc = fsw/10, and Bode plot of loop gain targets. Format as calculations with numeric answers and BOM table in INR. Consider these constraints: assume Indian ambient 40 degC, components sourceable from Mouser India or Digi-Key India, and compliance with CE/EMI margins. My specs: [PASTE]

prompts.title: Op-Amp Circuit Analysis and Design
prompts.prompt: Act as an analog circuits professor with 20 years experience at IIT Kanpur teaching EE331 Analog. I need you to analyze or design an op-amp circuit based on my inputs. Here is the context: I am a 4th-semester EEE student at NIT Rourkela. I will either paste a circuit (topology, resistor values, op-amp model like LM358, OP07, LM324, TL072) or give me a functional requirement (inverting, non-inverting, summer, difference, integrator, differentiator, instrumentation amp). Please provide: (1) under ideal op-amp assumptions (infinite gain, zero input current, virtual short), derive the closed-form output expression step by step, (2) non-ideal analysis: effect of finite GBW, input offset voltage (Vos), input bias current (Ib), CMRR for the chosen op-amp from the datasheet, (3) frequency response including the GBW-limited -3 dB bandwidth, (4) stability with capacitive load if relevant, (5) component selection from Indian distributors (Robu, Element14 India) with INR costing, (6) a LTspice or Python snippet simulating the DC and AC response. Format as equations, tables, and code. Consider these constraints: assume +/-12V or single 5V supply as per typical Indian lab, and mention rail-to-rail limitation of LM358.

prompts.title: BJT Biasing and Small-Signal Model
prompts.prompt: Act as an electronic devices and circuits lecturer with 12 years experience at Jadavpur University. I need you to compute the DC operating point and small-signal parameters of a BJT amplifier I describe. Here is the context: I am a 3rd-semester EEE student at IIEST Shibpur. I will give the topology (CE with voltage-divider bias, CB, CC/emitter follower), VCC, resistor values (R1, R2, RC, RE, RL), coupling caps, and transistor parameters (BC547, 2N2222 typical beta 100 to 300, VBE 0.7V). Please provide: (1) Thevenin equivalent of base bias, (2) DC analysis: IB, IC, IE, VCE, VC, VE, VB; verify active region VCE > VCE(sat), (3) small-signal parameters: gm = IC/VT, r_pi = beta/gm, ro = VA/IC (assume VA = 100V if not given), (4) mid-band small-signal model and computation of Av, Ai, Rin, Rout, (5) effect of emitter bypass capacitor removal on gain and Rin, (6) a Python snippet computing the Q-point and gain, plus SPICE netlist for cross-check. Format as equations and tables with numeric values. Consider these constraints: use Sedra-Smith notation commonly used in Indian curricula, VT = 26 mV at room temperature, and flag thermal runaway risk. My circuit: [PASTE]

prompts.title: MOSFET Amplifier DC and AC Analysis
prompts.prompt: Act as an analog IC designer with 11 years experience at Analog Devices Bengaluru. I need you to analyze a MOSFET amplifier circuit I provide, computing the DC bias point and small-signal gain. Here is the context: I am a 5th-sem EEE student at NIT Suratkal. I will give the topology (CS, CG, CD source follower), supply VDD, resistor values, and MOSFET parameters (Kn = un*Cox*W/L in mA/V^2, VTH, lambda). Please provide: (1) DC analysis assuming saturation: ID = (Kn/2)*(VGS-VTH)^2*(1+lambda*VDS), solve for VGS and ID simultaneously and verify saturation (VDS > VGS-VTH), (2) VD, VS, VDS and gm = sqrt(2*Kn*ID), ro = 1/(lambda*ID), (3) small-signal mid-band voltage gain Av, Rin, Rout for the topology, (4) effect of body effect if source is not at ground, (5) frequency response estimate using the Miller effect on Cgd, (6) LTspice netlist using a generic NMOS model (e.g., CD4007 or BSS138) available in Indian labs. Format as equations, tables, and SPICE code. Consider these constraints: follow Razavi textbook conventions used in Indian M.Tech analog courses, and comment on the saturation assumption holding at the extremes. My circuit: [PASTE]

prompts.title: Digital Logic Boolean Minimization
prompts.prompt: Act as a digital electronics professor with 15 years experience at IIT Guwahati. I need you to minimize a Boolean function and draw the resulting circuit. Here is the context: I am a 3rd-sem EEE student at NIT Agartala. I will give the function as a sum-of-minterms, product-of-maxterms, or truth table with up to 5 variables and possibly don't-cares. Please provide: (1) truth table listing with the output column, (2) K-map (Karnaugh map) for 3, 4, or 5 variables drawn in ASCII with groupings highlighted using letters (A, B, C ...), (3) minimized SOP and POS expressions, (4) Quine-McCluskey tabular method for verification showing prime implicants and essential prime implicants, (5) gate-level realization using NAND-only and NOR-only forms with gate count, (6) implementation using a 4:1 or 8:1 MUX plus a decoder approach, which Indian VTU and Anna Univ exams frequently ask. Format as tables, K-map diagrams, and logic expressions. Consider these constraints: follow Morris Mano textbook used at most Indian universities, highlight any hazards (static-1 hazard) and the covering term needed to eliminate them. My function: [PASTE]

prompts.title: Verilog Module for FSM Design
prompts.prompt: Act as an ASIC verification engineer with 10 years experience at Qualcomm India. I need you to write synthesizable Verilog for a finite state machine I describe. Here is the context: I am a 7th-semester EEE student at IIIT Bangalore doing a VLSI design lab. I will describe the FSM (sequence detector for 1011 overlapping, traffic light controller for an Indian 4-way junction, vending machine, or similar), number of states, inputs, outputs, and whether Mealy or Moore. Please provide: (1) state transition diagram in text with states and transitions labelled, (2) state encoding choice (binary, Gray, one-hot) with rationale, (3) complete Verilog module using the 2-always or 3-always block style (state register, next-state logic, output logic), (4) synchronous reset, single clock, (5) a self-checking testbench using $display and $monitor that exercises all transitions, (6) expected simulation waveform annotation and tips for running in ModelSim/Xilinx Vivado or the free EDA Playground. Format as Verilog code blocks. Consider these constraints: code must be synthesizable (avoid initial blocks for state, avoid blocking assignments in sequential), follow Samir Palnitkar style used in Indian textbooks. My FSM spec: [PASTE]

prompts.title: VHDL Counter and Testbench
prompts.prompt: Act as an FPGA instructor with 9 years experience at CDAC Pune running VLSI training programs. I need you to write a clean VHDL module for a parameterized counter and a self-checking testbench. Here is the context: I am a 6th-semester EEE student at PEC Chandigarh. I want an N-bit up/down counter with synchronous load, enable, asynchronous reset, and terminal count output. Please provide: (1) library includes (ieee, std_logic_1164, numeric_std), (2) entity with generic N (default 8), ports clk, rst_n, en, up_dn, load, d_in, q_out, tc, (3) architecture using a process with rising_edge(clk) and proper async reset, (4) testbench that instantiates the counter, generates a 100 MHz clock, and checks 30+ stimulus cases including loading a value, counting up to terminal count, counting down, and assert-based checks, (5) instructions to simulate on Xilinx Vivado (Nexys A7 board common in Indian VLSI labs) or ModelSim, (6) synthesis-friendly coding notes. Format as two code blocks (DUT and TB) with comments. Consider these constraints: strict numeric_std usage (no std_logic_arith), IEEE 1076-2008 compliant, and follow the coding style preferred by Indian PSU interviewers.

prompts.title: 8051 Microcontroller Assembly Program
prompts.prompt: Act as a microcontroller lab instructor with 18 years experience at Osmania University teaching 8051 assembly. I need you to write well-commented 8051 assembly (for Keil uVision C51) for a classroom assignment I describe. Here is the context: I am a 5th-sem EEE student at CBIT Hyderabad. I will specify the task (blink an LED on P1.0 at 1 Hz using Timer 0, read a switch on P3.2 and toggle an LED, 4x4 keypad scan, LCD 16x2 4-bit interfacing, serial transmission at 9600 baud with XTAL 11.0592 MHz). Please provide: (1) memory map and port usage summary, (2) equates and ORG statements for reset and interrupt vectors, (3) main routine with initialization and a clean loop, (4) subroutines for delay, ISR, or LCD command/data as needed, (5) a line-by-line English comment on every instruction, (6) expected output and how to test on a Proteus simulation (widely used in Indian labs) or an actual 8051 trainer kit like Vi Microsystems. Format as a Keil-compatible .a51 code block. Consider these constraints: AT89C51/AT89S52 (Atmel 8051), XTAL = 11.0592 MHz unless stated, and follow the Mazidi textbook used in most Indian universities. My task: [DESCRIBE]

prompts.title: Solar PV System Sizing for Indian Home
prompts.prompt: Act as a rooftop solar design engineer with 12 years experience at Tata Power Solar designing kW-scale systems across Indian states. I need you to size a grid-tied rooftop solar PV system for a specific Indian home based on inputs I provide. Here is the context: I am a 7th-semester EEE student at COEP Pune doing my mini-project. I will give the city (affects solar insolation in kWh/m2/day), monthly electricity bill in INR, DISCOM tariff slab, roof area available in sqm, and shading notes. Please provide: (1) estimated daily and monthly kWh consumption from the bill using the DISCOM slab structure (MSEDCL/BESCOM/TNEB tariffs), (2) system size in kWp using local insolation (Pune 5.2, Bangalore 5.5, Chennai 5.3, Delhi 4.8 kWh/m2/day) and 75 percent performance ratio, (3) number of panels (assume 540 W mono-PERC panels from Waaree or Adani) and string configuration for an on-grid inverter (Sungrow/Growatt 3-5 kW typical), (4) roof area check and structure orientation (south-facing, 15-20 deg tilt for Indian latitudes), (5) net-metering impact on bill and payback period using MNRE subsidy (up to 3 kW: 40 percent, 3-10 kW: 20 percent as of 2024 PM Surya Ghar scheme), (6) CEA safety compliance and IS 16221 module standard notes. Format as a calculation table and BOM in INR. Consider these constraints: honour MNRE guidelines and DISCOM approval process. My inputs: [PASTE]

prompts.title: Earthing System Design Per IS 3043
prompts.prompt: Act as an electrical installation consultant with 20 years experience designing earthing systems for Indian industrial plants compliant with IS 3043 and CEA regulations. I need you to help me design an earthing system for a scenario I describe. Here is the context: I am a final-year EEE student at NIT Silchar doing an industrial visit report on an 11kV/415V substation. I will give soil resistivity in ohm-m (typical Indian values 50-500), fault current level in kA, fault clearing time, and available area. Please provide: (1) computation of required earth electrode resistance target (typically less than 1 ohm for generating stations, 2 ohms for substations, 4 ohms for lightning, 8 ohms for domestic per IS 3043), (2) sizing of earth conductor using I*sqrt(t)/k formula per IS 3043 with copper/GI k values, (3) choice of pipe, plate, strip or chemical earthing and number of electrodes, (4) step and touch voltage check per IEEE 80 / IS 3043 Annex, (5) layout sketch in text of the earth grid, (6) maintenance and testing schedule (fall-of-potential, earth continuity) with instruments like Megger DET4TC. Format as a calculation section and a compliance checklist. Consider these constraints: IS 3043:2018, CEA Safety Regulations 2010, and Indian soil characteristics. My inputs: [PASTE]

prompts.title: GATE EE Numerical Practice Set
prompts.prompt: Act as a GATE EE coach with 12 years experience at ACE Engineering Academy Hyderabad whose students consistently score AIR under 100. I need you to generate a high-quality practice set on a specific GATE EE topic I name. Here is the context: I am a final-year EEE student at IIT ISM Dhanbad targeting a top-100 rank in GATE 2026. I will name the topic (e.g., network theory, electromagnetics, signals and systems, control systems, electrical machines, power systems, power electronics, analog, digital, measurements, engineering math). Please provide: (1) 10 numerical answer type (NAT) questions of increasing difficulty with realistic GATE-level twists, (2) 5 multiple choice questions with 4 options each, (3) complete step-by-step solutions for all, (4) a one-paragraph concept recap of the most tested formulas for that topic, (5) common traps (sign convention, units, pu base change) that cost students marks, (6) 3 past-year GATE reference problem numbers (IIT set paper, year) mapped to each concept if possible. Format as a question bank followed by a solutions section. Consider these constraints: exact GATE difficulty calibration (2021-2024 trend), strict dimensional analysis, and times each question with a 2-3 minute target. Topic: [NAME TOPIC]

prompts.title: Final Year EEE Project Proposal
prompts.prompt: Act as a project guide with 15 years experience mentoring final-year EEE projects at VJTI Mumbai, many of which won IEEE student paper awards. I need you to write a full project proposal for a topic idea I provide. Here is the context: I am a final-year EEE student with a team of 3 and a budget of Rs 8000. I will give the project theme (IoT energy meter, EV charger, BLDC motor controller, grid-tied inverter, power quality monitor, smart home, or similar). Please provide: (1) title and abstract (200 words), (2) literature survey referencing 5 recent IEEE papers (2022-2024) with brief notes on each, (3) block diagram in text, (4) detailed hardware BOM with Indian distributor (Robu.in, Element14 India) part numbers and INR prices totaling under Rs 8000, (5) software stack and algorithms with a short flowchart in text, (6) weekly Gantt chart covering 16 weeks (typical Indian 8th sem duration), (7) expected results and novelty statement, (8) possible extensions for a conference paper at ICIEEE or NPEC. Format as a structured proposal document. Consider these constraints: feasible on undergraduate budget, compliant with IS safety standards, and suitable for a panel evaluation common in Indian universities. Project theme: [PASTE]

prompts.title: EEE Core Placement Interview Prep
prompts.prompt: Act as an HR and technical interviewer with 15 years experience hiring EEE graduates at BHEL, NTPC, Siemens India and ABB India. I need you to prepare me thoroughly for a core EEE placement interview. Here is the context: I am a final-year student at NIT Kurukshetra with a CGPA of 8.5, branch EEE, projects in power electronics and FPGA, and I am targeting BHEL ET, NTPC ET, PGCIL ET, Siemens GET, ABB GET. I will paste my resume summary. Please provide: (1) 20 core technical questions across machines, power systems, power electronics, control, and measurements with model answers (3-5 lines each), (2) 10 HR questions (why core, why this company, strengths/weaknesses) with sample answers tailored to Indian PSU and MNC culture, (3) 5 situational questions (a line trip in a 400 kV substation, VFD failure on shop floor) with a STAR-format answer, (4) a checklist of formulas and single-line diagrams to revise the night before, (5) dress code and etiquette specific to PSU interviews vs MNC GET, (6) a list of recent Indian power sector developments (Green Hydrogen Mission, RDSS, PM Surya Ghar) to discuss. Format as Q and A sections. Consider these constraints: emphasize CEA grid code awareness and Atmanirbhar Bharat initiatives. My resume: [PASTE]

prompts.title: Measurement Lab Report Writer
prompts.prompt: Act as an electrical measurements lab instructor with 14 years experience at Delhi Technological University. I need you to write a complete, exam-ready lab report for an EEE measurements experiment I describe. Here is the context: I am a 4th-semester EEE student at DTU. I will give the experiment (calibration of single-phase energy meter, measurement of three-phase power by two-wattmeter method, measurement of low/medium/high resistance using Kelvin double bridge or Wheatstone or loss of charge, measurement of inductance using Maxwell bridge, Anderson bridge, or Hay bridge, CRO-based measurements, or instrument transformer testing). Please provide: (1) aim, (2) apparatus table with instrument ranges and least counts (MECO, Rishabh, Automatic Electric typical Indian lab makes), (3) theory with governing equations derived (balance condition for bridges), (4) neat circuit diagram in text, (5) step-by-step procedure, (6) sample observation table with 5 readings and calculations, (7) percentage error and sources of error, (8) precautions and result. Format as a standard Indian engineering college lab report. Consider these constraints: IS standards for accuracy class (0.5, 1.0, 2.0 class meters), mention calibration against a reference standard, and follow the exact structure expected by Indian lab examiners. Experiment: [NAME]
